sourceMovie.title: Corpse Bride
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Corpse Bride works as a romance movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The romance and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Beauty and the Beast
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Beauty and the Beast shares Corpse Bride's gift for romance — the 1991 film delivers fantasy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Hotel Transylvania 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Corpse Bride compelling — the fantasy, the animation — shows up in Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Ghost
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The romance energy of Corpse Bride is alive in Ghost (1990), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Meet Joe Black
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Meet Joe Black (1998) operates in the same romance as Corpse Bride, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The bittersweet payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Illusionist
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Illusionist (2006) belongs to the same conversation as Corpse Bride — both anchor their romance in fantasy emotion that doesn't let go. The bittersweet undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Penelope
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Corpse Bride's romance stayed with you, Penelope (2007) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The fantasy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Beetlejuice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Corpse Bride excels in fantasy, Beetlejuice (1988) doubles down on comedy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Lobster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Lobster (2015) is the natural next step after Corpse Bride: same romance DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and comedy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Addams Family
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Corpse Bride who respond to its fantasy will find The Addams Family (1991) immediately familiar. Both films share a comedy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: What Women Want
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Corpse Bride fans keep landing on What Women Want (2000) — the romance is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

faq.question: What should I watch after Corpse Bride?
faq.answer: Try Beauty and the Beast, Hotel Transylvania 2, Ghost—each shares DNA with Corpse Bride while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Corpse Bride?
faq.answer: Corpse Bride (2005) is best described as Romance, Fantasy, Animation. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Corpse Bride worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
